title: Male/Female Octave FAIL: falsifier with wrong female transform and wrong product ratio
_primary_source_note: Intentional falsifier for validator testing (not a new claim), reusing the same algebraic identity as the PASS fixtures. Grounded per the same sources as the PASS fixtures: Ben Iverson QA framework (Iverson, 1991) male/female QN taxonomy; Dale Pond SVP (Pond, 2004) Keely male/female vibration terminology.
_fixture_purpose: Falsifier: declares a female QN that does not follow the (2e,b,a,2d) transform and whose product is not 4x the male product. NOTE: 'result' is deliberately 'PASS' here (not 'FAIL') because this validator's validate() short-circuits to skip all pair-checking logic when result=='FAIL' -- setting 'PASS' lets MF_TRANS/MF_PROD actually run and catch the planted defects, matching the file's real intended validation OUTCOME (should FAIL under --self-test's expectation).

algebraic_proof.claim: female_product = 4 x male_product for any QN (b,e,d,a) with d=b+e, a=b+2e
algebraic_proof.transform: female = (b_f, e_f, d_f, a_f) = (2e, b, a, 2d)
steps: Same algebraic identity as the PASS fixtures; this fixture intentionally violates it to test detection.

pairs.check: fundamental pair, correct -- kept valid so this fixture isolates the defect to the second pair
FALSIFIER: correct female should be (b_f=2e=4, e_f=b=1, d_f=a=5, a_f=2d=6), product=4x30=120. Declared (5,1,6,7) product=210 violates both MF_TRANS and MF_PROD.

connections.qa_source: Same as PASS fixtures; this is an intentional falsifier for validator testing, not a new claim.

validation_checks.description: female=(2e,b,a,2d) -- FALSIFIER: second pair violates this
female product=4xmale product -- FALSIFIER: second pair is 7x not 4x
2 pairs (below the >=3 minimum, also caught by MF_W)
